articles.headline: World Cup Daily Live: Belgium face off with Egypt; Cape Verde shock Spain in draw
articles.description: Enjoy all the action from Monday's World Cup matches, with features, news, video analysis and color from around the competition.

articles.headline: Spain frustrated by heroic Cape Verde in World Cup opener

images.name: Cape Verde [1296x729]
images.caption: Cape Verde goalkeeper Vozinha impressed against Spain.
images.alt: Cape Verde goalkeeper Vozinha makes a save against Spain.
images.credit: (Photo by Roberto SCHMIDT / AFP via Getty Images,

images.name: Burley: Cape Verde couldn't have given a better account of themselves
images.caption: Burley: Cape Verde couldn't have given a better account of themselves

articles.headline: Why are there hydration breaks at the 2026 World Cup, and why has FIFA been criticised?
articles.description: All 104 matches at the 2026 World Cup are stopping for two hydration breaks. Why have they been stipulated by FIFA, and why have they been criticised?

images.name:
Players of Tunisia take drinks at the hydration break during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group F match between Sweden and Tunisia at Monterrey Stadium
 [1296x729]
images.alt: Players of Tunisia take drinks at the hydration break during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group F match between Sweden and Tunisia at Monterrey Stadium

images.name: Enzo Fernandez #8 of Chelsea FC drinks water in a hydration break during the FIFA Club World Cup 2025 semi-final match between Fluminense FC and Chelsea FC at MetLife Stadium [1296x729]
images.caption: Argentina's Enzo Fernández said he felt "dizzy" while playing in "very dangerous" temperatures during last summer's Club World Cup in the United States.
images.alt: Enzo Fernandez #8 of Chelsea FC drinks water in a hydration break during the FIFA Club World Cup 2025 semi-final match between Fluminense FC and Chelsea FC at MetLife Stadium

images.name:
Electronic screen displaying hydration break information during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group C match between Haiti and Scotland at Boston Stadium
 [1296x729]
images.caption: Advertising is prominent on stadium screens during drinks breaks at the World Cup.
images.alt: Electronic screen displaying hydration break information during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group C match between Haiti and Scotland at Boston Stadium

images.name:
Virgil van Dijk (Netherlands) looks on during Group F FIFA World Cup 2026, Netherlands and Japan, Dallas Stadium
 [1296x729]
images.caption: Virgil van Dijk has been critical of the hydration breaks introduced at this summer's World Cup.
images.alt: Virgil van Dijk (Netherlands) looks on during Group F FIFA World Cup 2026, Netherlands and Japan, Dallas Stadium

images.name:
The Brazil team talk during a hydration break during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group C match between Brazil and Morocco at New York New Jersey Stadium
 [1296x729]
images.caption: Carlo Ancelotti was thankful for the opportunity to speak to his players when Brazil found themselves a goal behind to Morocco in the first half of their World Cup opener.
images.alt: The Brazil team talk during a hydration break during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group C match between Brazil and Morocco at New York New Jersey Stadium

images.name:
Myung-Bo Hong, Head Coach of Korea Republic, gives his players instructions during a hydration break during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group A match between Korea Republic and Czechia
 [1296x729]
images.caption: Hydration breaks provide a window for coaches to gather their players and tweak their tactics.
images.alt: Myung-Bo Hong, Head Coach of Korea Republic, gives his players instructions during a hydration break during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group A match between Korea Republic and Czechia

images.name:
Germany's forward #26 Deniz Undav, Germany's midfielder #13 Pascal Gross, Germany's midfielder #17 Florian Wirtz, Germany's midfielder #05 Aleksandar Pavlovic and teammates celebrate
 [1296x729]
images.caption: Drinks breaks could have a major effect on the outcome of matches at the World Cup.
images.alt: Germany's forward #26 Deniz Undav, Germany's midfielder #13 Pascal Gross, Germany's midfielder #17 Florian Wirtz, Germany's midfielder #05 Aleksandar Pavlovic and teammates celebrate
images.credit: Kirill KUDRYAVTSEV / AFP via Getty Images

commentary.text: Our preview picks out Eren Derdiyok as being crucial to this game as Switzerland aim to secure a place in the second round.
Join us at 18.30 GMT to see if the Swiss can sneak ahead of either Spain or Chile.

Switzerland have a huge chance to progress tonight. If Switzerland can win this game by two goals they are through, no matter what happens between Spain and Chile. Against an uninspiring Honduras team, they must be confident of making the last 16.
For Switzerland, Erin Derdiyok replaces Alexander Frei up front, while Tranquillo Barnetta replaces the suspended Valon Behrami.
Honduras have made six changes, Jerry Palacios joins his brother Wilson in the starting 11. Emilio Izaguirre and Danilo Turcios are dropped, veteran striker Carlos Pavon remains on the bench.
The teams are out at the Free State Stadium, almost time to get this one under way.

ESPN Stats & Info: During their match against Chile, Switzerland set a new record for consecutive minutes without conceding at the World Cup finals with 558 straight scoreless minutes. Honduras have failed to score in their last three World Cup games.

ESPN Stats & Info: Against Chile, Switzerland had the worst passing accuracy of any team in a World Cup game since 1966 (56.7%). After the first two rounds of matches, the Swiss have completed fewer passes than any other side (282).
ESPN Stats & Info: Honduras have managed just two shots on target this World Cup, fewest of any team.
KICK OFF Switzerland start the match!
Although Honduras still have an incredibly slim chance of qualifying, this game is all about Switzerland. Can they get the two goal win they need?
Honduras have started brightly, looking energetic and certainly up for the challenge.
Hendry Thomas is the first man to be booked for a late challenge on Gokhan Inler.
Switzerland have adopted a defensive approach to their opening two games, surely they can't do that today against a team they really should be beating?
Huggel is down and in some pain after a crunching tackle from Wilson Palacios. The Spurs man is lucky to stay out of the book.
It's a slow start for Switzerland, out of the two teams Honduras have looked more adventurous, without creating any chances as yet.
Nunez with a quick break down the Honduras right, his cross is blocked and Palacios can't control the ball and it's a Swiss throw in.
First shot of the game for Switzerland, it was worringly easy for them to get through Honduras then. Barnetta and Inler linked up well only for Inler to drag a low shot wide.
A slightly lacklustre start from Switzerland, hopefully that last attack will have woken them up.
Nunez brings down Lichtsteiner, free kick to Switzerland taken short and worked back to Benaglio. Switzerland had a chance to put the ball into the Honduras penalty are from that set piece.
Nearly a chance for David Suazo, sloppy pass from Barnetta, Hendry Thomas slides the ball through for Suazo to chase but it's just too long for the Inter man.
Lichsteiner gets deep into Honduras' half, he cuts the ball across the penalty area, where there wasn't a single Swiss player. Hitzfeld's men need to show more ambition.
First chance for Switzerland. Tranquillo Barnetta shapes an excellent ball across the six-yard box only for Derdiyok to guide his header wide. That should've been 1-0.
David Suazo looks to have the pace to trouble the Swiss defence. Another through ball finds him just offside.
Lichsteiner is looking to get forward at every opportunity for Switzerland. The ball out to him is over hit and it's a Honduras throw.
Benaglio is out of his goal quickly to clear a long clearance. Switzerland really have to be aware of Suazo's pace.
Derdiyok had an excellent chance to put N'Kufo through, his control is poor and the opportunity passes.
Barnetta unleashes a 25-yard effort that Valladares has to gather at the second attempt.
Inler bundles over Bernandez and Honduras have a free kick in their own half. This game needs a goal.
A game that does have a goal is Spain against Chile. David Villa has made it 1-0 to Spain.
Honduras waste an excellent opportunity to break. The ball through to Jonny Palacios is hugely over hit. Palacios' ball in is a poor one and the danger passes.
Switzerland have their first corner after another dash forward from Lichsteiner.
The corner is comfortably dealt with. The delivery from set pieces has been poor at this tournament. They do have another chance though, with Barnetta over a free kick 40 yards out.
Barnetta's ball in causes momentary panic, but Honduras regain their composure and punt it clear.
Honduras have a free kick of their own, which they also waste. The ball is worked out to Alvarez who is in an offside position.
Barnetta has a free after his in dragged down by Sabillon. It's in an almost identical position to his last attempt.
Derdiyok gets his head to the cross, Grichting knocks the lose ball harmlessly out of play for a goal kick.
Gelson Fernandes booked for taking down Wilson Palacios as he raced clear. Cynical from the former Manchester City man.
A terrible effort from Bernardez. From fully 40 yards he balloons the ball way over the Swiss crossbar.
It's 2-0 to Spain through Andres Iniesta, it doesn't change Switzerland's position much. A two goal win sends them through regardless.
Chile are also down to ten men in that game, a real up hill struggle for them now.
This game needs a goal to bring it to life, it's gone a little bit flat.
Barnetta makes a positive run forward and earns a corner for his troubles. Can they create a chance?

Inexplicably N'Kufo fails to shoot at the back post. Derdiyok works the ball around Sabillon and puts a fantastic ball across the box. N'Kufo tries to chest the ball down and wastes a great opportunity. Will we see Alexander Frei in the second half?
Ziegler over another set piece. His ball in is much better and N'Kufo misses another opportunity as he heads wide from six yards.
ONE ADDED MINUTE
HALF TIME
Switzerland really need to up the tempo here. Hitzfeld needs to remind his team what's at stake.

ESPN Stats & Info: After the first two rounds of matches, Honduras had put only 12.5% of their shots on target (two of 16), which was the lowest percentage for any team. None of Honduras' two chances so far today have been on target.

Honduras seem to be lacking belief. They've had slight chances on the break, but seem to make the wrong decisions or misplace the final ball. With a little more composure they could potentially pose a threat in the second half.

ESPN Stats & Info: If results hold, this would be the first World Cup under the current 32-team format in which no more than one European team advances from any group. Five groups had two European nations this year.
Hakan Yakin on for Gelson Fernandes
KICK OFF Honduras restart the match.
Will the introduction of Yakin give Switzerland a more incisive attack?
As it stands, Switzerland are heading out. A win here, and they would advance with Spain at the expense of Chile.
Poor from Alvarez. Stood on the touchline, looking down the defensive line and the Honduras winger still managers to wonder offside.
Jerry Palacios and Grichting are down after a thundering clash of heads. Palacios is up, Grichting is still on his back. This doesn't look good.
Chile have pulled a goal back against Spain, it's now 2-1. Meanwhile Grichting is being put on a stretcher, but he is sat up which is great news.
One goal is no longer enough for Switzerland. They must win by a two goal margin if Spain only win by a one goal margin. It's very simple....honest.
One goal is no longer enough for Switzerland. They must win by a two goal margin if Spain only win by a one goal margin. It's very simple....honest.
What a chance for David Suazo! Alvarez does wonderfully down the right, his glorious ball is right on Suazo's forehead but he can only guide it wide. Best chance of the game by a mile.
Hakan Yakin has an attempt on goal from 30 yards, it's on target and comfortably saved.
Switzerland need to really put Honduras under pressure. They're letting this slip away too easily.
A beautifully weighted ball through from Yakin, N'Kufo seemed to be through until he tangled with Chavez, who has stayed down.
Chavez is back on his feet, luckily the stretcher bearers aren't required.
David Suazo gets his name taken for a high foot on Grichting.
ESPN Stats & Info: As of right now, the 2.1 goals-per-match average is the lowest in any World Cup.

A quick break from Honduras, Nunez with a pacy run forward, the ball is drilled into the box straight at Benaglio. Barnetta has a chance at the other end, he had Yakin free outside of him, ignored him and hit a tame shot in the grasp of Valladares.

Honduras are looking far more dangerous than Switzerland, Nunez looking lively again, his cross just misses Jerry Palacios. Derdiyok has a chance in response, drilling his 12 yard effort down the throat of Valladares.
Osman Chavez booked for hacking down Derdiyok on the edge of the box. A real chance for Switzerland...
After a long wait, Yakin disappoints. His free kick hits Bernardez and goes out for a corner.
Walter Martinez on for Ramon Nunez
Ziegler's corner is hit long to Yakin, he chests it down, his touch is heavy though and the chance passes.
Yakin is brought down and it's another chance from a free kick. Ziegler over this one.
Ziegler's delivery is cleared by Chavez, the return ball in is flicked on by Deridiyok, just too far for N'Kufo.
Alexander Frei on for Blaise N'Kufo
Gelson Fernandes and Edgar Alvarez fight for the ball
Swiss corner after a good run from Derdiyok. They need a goal soon. Remember as things stand Switzerland need a two goal win.

Benaglio keeps Switzerland in the World Cup! Honduras break from the corner, Suazo slips in Alvarez he curls his effort towards the top corner. Benaglio produces an excellent save to deny him. The corner is taken short and comes to nothing.
Alexander Frei blazes over from inside the box. Valladares fumbles a ball into the box. It's cut back to Frei who can't keep his shot under the crossbar.

Switzerland are looking more dangerous. A much better delivery from Yakin, his bending ball finds Frei at the back post. He elects to go with his foot instead of his head and can't direct it on target.
Good covering from Ziegler, cutting across Jerry Palacios and conceding the corner.
The corner is dealt with by Switzerland and they look to attack.
Xherdan Shaqiri on for Benjamin Huggel
Georgie Welcome on for Jerry Palacios
It doesn't look like it's going to be Switzerland's night. A quick break slows down, resulting in Lichtsteiner lashing a wild shot over. Switzerland still need two.
Another Yakin free kick works its way to Derdiyok, his low ball in is poor and easily cleared.
Valladares comes and claims a corner excellently, punching clear and Honduras can break.
The ball is back with Switzerland, an wicked ball is hit across goal by Frei and Yakin can't get on the end of it.
ESPN Stats & Info: If the scoreline holds, this would be the first 0-0 draw in Honduras? World Cup history. It would be the third for the Swiss. Both of their previous two came in the 2006 World Cup.
Honduras have the ball in the net, but it's clearly offside. Switzerland are looking to go long now.
The Honduras keeper is down after making an excellent save from Frei. Shame for him it didn't matter as Frei was offside. To top it off he injured himself in the fall.
Danilo Turcios on for David Suazo
Yakin has looked lively since he came on. He instigates a spell of quick passing which results in a poor cross.
Chance for Switzerland, and a chance for Yakin...
Wilson Palacios booked for bringing down Shaqiri, a real chance here.
Yakin's set piece is straight at the wall. Switzerland have not been good enough and defeat would be harsh on Honduras.
FOUR MINUTES ADDED TIME

Georgie Welcome almost wins it for Honduras! With Switzerland committed forward, he breaks clear, his side-footed effort is deflected over the bar. The corner goes right across the six-yard box with no one getting a touch.
Yakin gets a header on target, but straight at Valladares. Switzerland have been poor.
Honduras should'be won this game in the closing stages. They have a break with five on three, the final ball is poor and nothing comes of it.
FULL TIME These two are eliminated with Chile and Spain advancing. Switzerland too negative and offered no attacking threat. Honduras were spirited, and should've made more of their late chances.
In truth, from a neutral point of view, Spain and Chile will be far more entertaining to watch than these two. Especially Switzerland.
Read our full match report here.
Switzerland will look back at a chance missed here. Honduras were happy play on the counter attack, and Switzerland never really gave it a go.
